serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of sigara boregi consists of 3 cigar-shaped fried pastry rolls, weighing approximately 90 g in total.

nutrient_source.title: Borek, fried, with cheese filling — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods composite; cross-referenced with Turkish food composition data (Beslenme Bilgi Sistemi, BeBiS) for fried cheese-filled filo pastry
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / BeBiS Turkish Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA entry for sigara boregi. Values adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for fried cheese-filled filo/phyllo pastry (NDB entries for phyllo dough, feta cheese, and frying oil) combined with published Turkish food composition data for borek with white cheese. Energy and macros reflect a fried filo pastry with approximately 30% cheese filling by weight. Values are consistent with peer-reviewed literature on Turkish borek composition.

gi_source.title: Phyllo/filo pastry and fried pastry GI estimation based on white wheat flour-based fried dough products; analogous reference: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for sigara boregi in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 57 (medium) based on analogous fried white-flour pastry products. Filo/phyllo dough is a refined wheat flour product; frying increases fat content and moderately lowers GI relative to baked equivalents. Glycemic load per 90 g serving calculated as (57 × 27.0 g available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = 15.4. Available carbohydrate per serving = 30.0 g/100g × 0.90 × 100 = 27.0 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (480 mg per 100 g; approximately 432 mg per 90 g serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit sigara boregi intake due to its high sodium content from cheese and added salt; a single serving provides approximately 18–22% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 57) and moderate glycemic load per serving (GL 15.4) from refined filo pastry
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume sigara boregi in moderation; the refined wheat filo pastry contributes a medium GI of approximately 57 and a glycemic load of 15.4 per 90 g serving, which can raise postprandial blood glucose if portion size is not controlled.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (6.5 g/100 g), trans fat (0.2 g/100 g), and high sodium from deep-frying and cheese filling
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit sigara boregi due to its combination of saturated fat, trans fat from frying, and high sodium, all of which are associated with increased LDL cholesterol and blood pressure risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sigara boregi (also spelled sigara böreği or sigara borek) is a traditional Turkish fried pastry snack made by rolling thin filo (phyllo) dough sheets around a filling of white cheese — typically feta or a similar brined curd cheese — mixed with fresh parsley, then deep-frying until golden and crisp. The name literally means 'cigarette pastry' in Turkish, referring to the slim cylindrical shape of each roll. The exterior is shatteringly crisp and golden-brown, while the interior is soft, salty, and creamy from the melted cheese. Sigara boregi is a staple of Turkish meze culture and is widely served as an appetiser, street food, or snack across Turkey and the broader Middle Eastern and Eastern Mediterranean region. In Singapore, it appears on the menus of Middle Eastern and Turkish restaurants. Nutritionally, sigara boregi is energy-dense at approximately 320 kcal per 100 g, with significant fat content (18 g/100 g, of which 6.5 g is saturated) from both the cheese filling and the deep-frying process. Sodium content is high at 480 mg per 100 g due to the brined cheese and added salt. The refined filo pastry contributes a medium glycemic index of approximately 57. Clinically, it is a concern for individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or Type 2 Diabetes due to its sodium, saturated fat, and refined carbohydrate content. Calcium content is notable at approximately 130 mg per 100 g from the cheese filling.

confidence_reason: No exact match found in a primary regional food composition database for sigara boregi as a prepared dish. Nutrient values adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for fried filo/phyllo pastry with cheese filling and cross-referenced with published Turkish food composition literature. GI is an analogous estimate based on fried white-flour pastry products from the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium rather than low because the ingredient composition is well-characterised and the adaptation methodology is defensible.
notes: Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source database for this food category. All other -1 values reflect nutrients not reported in the adapted USDA/BeBiS source for this fried pastry product. GI of 57 is an analogous estimate based on fried refined wheat flour pastry; no direct GI measurement for sigara boregi was found in the University of Sydney GI Database as of the accessed date. Glycemic load per 90 g serving = (57 × 27.0) / 100 = 15.4. The food is classified under Middle Eastern cuisine but originates specifically from Turkish culinary tradition. Multilingual names provided for Singaporean context (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) as the food is served in Singapore. Chinese names (烟卷酥饼 / 煙卷酥餅) are descriptive translations meaning 'cigarette crispy pastry'. Tamil and Hindi names are phonetic transliterations of the Turkish name. The halal flag is set to true as the standard recipe contains no pork or alcohol; however, restaurant-specific halal certification should be verified independently. Trans fat value of 0.2 g/100 g reflects minor trans fat from frying oil; this is an estimate and may vary by oil type used.
